candidates.title: Extract useBodyScrollLock

occurrences.snippet_summary: Inline `function useBodyScrollLock(active)` saves prior `document.body.style.overflow`, sets it to 'hidden' when active, restores on cleanup. Called as useBodyScrollLock(true) at line 31.

useEffect keyed on `[item, onClose]` saves originalOverflow, sets body.style.overflow='hidden' AND adds Escape keydown listener; cleanup restores both. Scroll-lock is conflated with the Escape handler in the same effect.

candidates.proposed_location: src/hooks/useBodyScrollLock.ts (currently a 0-line empty file — overwrite with real implementation)
key_behaviors_to_preserve: no-op when active is false (PostSection passes literal `true`; AiDevKitModal passes `!!item`)
snapshot previous document.body.style.overflow on activation
set document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden' while active
restore the previously snapshotted value on cleanup (NOT a hardcoded '' — the AiDevKitModal test at line 79-92 explicitly verifies the prior 'auto' value is restored)
candidates.risk_notes: Both consumers have tests. The AiDevKitModal effect must be split: extract scroll lock into the new hook, leave the Escape listener inline (or extract into a separate useEscapeKey hook — see hook-005). The two inlined implementations are behaviourally identical for the scroll-lock portion; previously-snapshotted-overflow restoration is already what both do.
tested_consumers: src/components/AiDevKitModal/index.tsx — has tests (index.test.tsx, lines 79-92 cover the lock/restore behavior)

candidates.title: Extract usePlaceholderImageFallback (or shared handleImageError helper)

occurrences.snippet_summary: handleImageError sets currentTarget.onerror = null; src = 'https://placehold.co/300x200/cccccc/333333?text=Image+Not+Found'
Same shape, fallback URL 300x300 with text=Image+Not+Found
Same shape, fallback URL 300x300 with text=Project+Image
Same shape, fallback URL 400x600 with text=Image+Not+Found
Same shape, fallback URL 250x400; ALSO calls setIsLoaded(true) — extra side effect beyond the URL swap
Slightly different — uses 'https://via.placeholder.com/250x150.png?text=Image+Not+Found' (DIFFERENT host: via.placeholder.com vs placehold.co) and does NOT clear onerror

candidates.proposed_location: src/utils/imageFallback.ts as `createImageFallbackHandler({ width, height, text, onAfter? })` returning a memoizable handler — a plain helper is more honest than a hook because the call sites just need a stable callback, not state.
candidates.proposed_signature: function createImageFallbackHandler(options: { width: number; height: number; text?: string; bg?: string; fg?: string; onAfter?: () => void }): (e: React.SyntheticEvent<HTMLImageElement>) => void
key_behaviors_to_preserve: Set e.currentTarget.onerror = null to prevent infinite loops
Substitute the placeholder URL with caller-provided dimensions / text
Optional onAfter callback — needed to preserve ProjectDetailPage's setIsLoaded(true) side effect
DECIDE during planning: standardize on placehold.co host (drop the lone via.placeholder.com usage in ProjectDetailComponent — it is the odd one out)
candidates.risk_notes: PortfolioCard and PreparingCard both have tests asserting placehold.co URLs; ProjectFlipPreviewCard / TotalProjectCard / ProjectDetailComponent / ProjectDetailPage do not. The ProjectDetailComponent variant uses a different host (via.placeholder.com) — Planner must decide whether to normalize. The ProjectDetailPage variant has the setIsLoaded side effect that the helper must support via callback.
tested_consumers: src/components/PortfolioCard/index.tsx — has tests (asserts 'placehold.co' in img.src on lines 56, 66 of index.test.tsx)
src/components/PreparingCard/index.tsx — has tests (asserts the exact Coming+Soon and Image+Not+Found URLs)

candidates.title: Extract useToggleSet for Set<string> add/delete toggle pattern

occurrences.snippet_summary: toggleHighlight + toggleSection — both setState((prev) => { const next = new Set(prev); if (next.has(key)) next.delete(key); else next.add(key); return next; })
toggleBlock, toggleIncludedProject, togglePanel — three identical implementations of the Set add/delete toggle pattern across selectedBlockIds, includedProjectIds, openPanels.

candidates.proposed_signature: function useToggleSet<T = string>(initial?: Iterable<T>): { items: Set<T>; toggle: (key: T) => void; has: (key: T) => boolean; reset: (next?: Iterable<T>) => void; }
key_behaviors_to_preserve: Functional setState with a fresh Set copy (do not mutate prev — React identity check requires a new reference)
If key present, delete; otherwise add
ResumePreviewPage's resetDraft (line 162-167) re-seeds selectedBlockIds and includedProjectIds from defaults — the hook must expose a `reset(next)` method so this stays one-line
candidates.risk_notes: ProfileSection has tests; ResumePreviewPage has no test file. Logic is mechanically identical across all 5 sites and trivially covered by a unit test on the new hook. Refactoring ResumePreviewPage's three toggles in lockstep needs care because the page is large (1048 lines).

candidates.title: Extract useExpandToggle for boolean expand/collapse useState

occurrences.snippet_summary: const [expanded, setExpanded] = useState(false); onClick={() => setExpanded(prev => !prev)}; aria-expanded={expanded}; renders ChevronIcon(expanded)
Identical shape — same useState+toggle+aria-expanded+ChevronIcon trio.
const [isFlipped, setIsFlipped] = useState(false); but driven by onMouseEnter→true / onMouseLeave→false — different semantics (hover-controlled, not toggle).

candidates.proposed_signature: function useDisclosure(initial = false): { isOpen: boolean; toggle: () => void; open: () => void; close: () => void; }
key_behaviors_to_preserve: Initial value defaults to false
toggle uses functional updater so callback identity-stable usage is safe
Provide explicit open/close so PortfolioCard's hover handlers (onMouseEnter→open, onMouseLeave→close) can adopt it cleanly
candidates.risk_notes: All three consumers (SkillGroups, DetailItems, PortfolioCard) have tests. The pattern is mechanical. PortfolioCard does not strictly need the hook (its naming `isFlipped` is domain-specific), but adopting useDisclosure unifies the API.

candidates.title: Extract useEscapeKey listener

occurrences.snippet_summary: Inline window.addEventListener('keydown', e => e.key === 'Escape' && onClose()) — bundled into the same effect as the body-scroll-lock.

candidates.proposed_signature: function useEscapeKey(handler: (() => void) | null | undefined, options?: { active?: boolean }): void
key_behaviors_to_preserve: no-op when handler is null/undefined or active=false
use latest-handler ref pattern so consumers do not need useCallback
attach to window keydown, dispose on cleanup
candidates.risk_notes: Currently only one consumer, but proposed alongside hook-001 because untangling AiDevKitModal's combined effect is the natural moment to lift this. Tested via AiDevKitModal/index.test.tsx (close-on-escape behavior verified).

candidates.title: Extract useClickOutside

occurrences.snippet_summary: useEffect attaches document mousedown listener while isDropdownOpen; closes dropdown when click target is outside dropdownRef. Standard outside-click pattern.

candidates.proposed_signature: function useClickOutside<T extends HTMLElement = HTMLElement>(ref: RefObject<T | null>, handler: () => void, active = true): void
key_behaviors_to_preserve: only attach the listener while active is true (PageHeader gates on isDropdownOpen — saves listener churn when closed)
use mousedown (not click) to fire before focus changes — preserve PageHeader semantics
remove listener on cleanup AND when active flips to false
candidates.risk_notes: Only one current consumer (PageHeader), which has tests. Listed because the pattern is well-known and any future modal/popover work will want it. If Planner is conservative about single-occurrence hooks, defer.

candidates.title: Extract useCopyToClipboardWithToast

occurrences.snippet_summary: useState toastMessage + linkCopySuccess; navigator.clipboard.writeText(...).then(success → set toast + setTimeout 3000ms clear).catch(failure → set failure toast + 3000ms clear). The 3-second auto-clear timer is duplicated across the success and failure branches.

candidates.proposed_signature: function useCopyToClipboardWithToast(options?: { successMessage?: string; failureMessage?: string; durationMs?: number }): { toastMessage: string; success: boolean | null; copy: (text: string) => Promise<void>; }
key_behaviors_to_preserve: navigator.clipboard.writeText guarded by promise chain (success vs failure)
toast auto-clears after durationMs (default 3000ms)
track success/failure flag separately from the message (TotalSummaryComponent already does)
cancel pending clear timer on unmount or on a subsequent copy() call so a stale timer cannot wipe a fresh toast
candidates.risk_notes: Single consumer today, but TotalSummaryComponent has tests (clipboard test on line 235 of TotalSummaryComponent.test.tsx flushes the resolved promise). The current inline code has a subtle issue worth fixing in the hook: rapid successive copy()s leave orphan setTimeouts that can clear a fresh toast — the hook should track and clear the prior timer.

candidates.title: Extract useFetchJson (or useLocalizedJson) for the fetch+useEffect+state triad

occurrences.snippet_summary: Module-level cachedLists + listsPromise + useState seed-from-cache pattern. Fetches /data/projects-list.json + /data/preparing-projects-list.json once.
Module-level cachedData + dataPromise + useEffect that hydrates state from the prefetched promise. Same shape as useProjectLists, fetches /data/introduction.json.

useEffect-based fetch of /data/introduction.json with try/catch and setError/setData. No prefetch cache — slightly different shape but same intent (and SAME endpoint as ProfileSection — see pattern observation).
Second useEffect: loads localized resume profile via loadLocalizedResumeProfile, keyed on i18n.language. Uses the isMounted guard pattern.
Same isMounted-guarded async load pattern — loadResumeBuilderData keyed on i18n.language, with loading/error states.

candidates.proposed_location: src/hooks/useFetchJson.ts (and/or src/hooks/useLocalizedResource.ts for the i18n-keyed variant)
candidates.proposed_signature: function useFetchJson<T>(url: string | (() => string), deps?: ReadonlyArray<unknown>): { data: T | null; isLoading: boolean; error: string | null; }
key_behaviors_to_preserve: isMounted guard so async resolution after unmount does not setState
loading/error/data triple state
for the i18n variants: re-fetch when i18n.resolvedLanguage / i18n.language changes

for the prefetch variants (useProjectLists, ProfileSection): module-level cache so navigation back to a section does not re-trigger a network request — this is a cross-cutting concern the hook needs to support OR the existing prefetch pattern stays intact and only InfoPost / ResumePreviewPage migrate
candidates.risk_notes: InfoPost has no tests AND its fetch logic is non-trivial (try/catch with two error paths). ResumePreviewPage has no tests. ProfileSection HAS tests but they likely mock the prefetch — verify before changing. Also: InfoPost duplicates ProfileSection's introduction.json fetch, so consolidating them is a behavioral change worth flagging separately. Recommend Planner DEFER this candidate; address it after writing tests for InfoPost + ResumePreviewPage. Listed here so it is not lost.
tested_consumers: src/hooks/useProjectLists.ts — indirectly tested via ProjectsSection tests (consumer is tested)

no_findings_areas: localStorage / sessionStorage usage — only i18n.ts touches localStorage (via i18next-browser-languagedetector caches: ['localStorage']); no app-side storage hook needed.

IntersectionObserver direct usage — only the existing src/hooks/useIntersectionObserver.ts contains a `new IntersectionObserver(...)` call, and no component currently consumes it; framer-motion's useInView (used in useProjectGridEntrance) covers the in-view detection needs. Recommend Planner consider DELETING the unused useIntersectionObserver.ts in a follow-up rather than extracting more.

3D card flip state — already lifted into useProjectFlipPreview for ProjectFlipPreviewCard; PortfolioCard's `isFlipped` is hover-driven and is better folded into useDisclosure (hook-004) than into the auto-cycling flip preview hook.

Toast / setTimeout-clear pattern outside of clipboard — no other components implement an auto-clearing toast; the only setTimeout-clear instance is the firework cleanup in DeveloperIntro (single occurrence, one-shot, not worth extracting).
Outside-click pattern outside of PageHeader — the AiDevKitModal closes via onClick on the backdrop (no document-level listener), so it does not match the useClickOutside shape.
